lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Will you think so?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Think so, Iago!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: What,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: To kiss in private?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: An unauthorized kiss.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Or to be naked with her friend in bed

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: An hour or more, not meaning any harm?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Naked in bed, Iago, and not mean harm!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: It is hypocrisy against the devil:

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: They that mean virtuously, and yet do so,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: The devil their virtue tempts, and they tempt heaven.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: So they do nothing, ’tis a venial slip:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: But if I give my wife a handkerchief—

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: What then?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Why, then, ’tis hers, my lord; and, being hers,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: She may, I think, bestow’t on any man.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: She is protectress of her honour too:

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: May she give that?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Her honour is an essence that’s not seen;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: They have it very oft that have it not:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: But, for the handkerchief—

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: By heaven, I would most gladly have forgot it.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Thou said’st, it comes o’er my memory,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: As doth the raven o’er the infected house,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Boding to all—he had my handkerchief.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Ay, what of that?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: That’s not so good now.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: What,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: If I had said I had seen him do you wrong?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Or heard him say—as knaves be such abroad,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Who having, by their own importunate suit,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Or voluntary dotage of some mistress,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Convinced or supplied them, cannot choose

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: But they must blab—

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Hath he said any thing?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: He hath, my lord; but be you well assured,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: No more than he’ll unswear.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: What hath he said?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: ’Faith, that he did—I know not what he did.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: What? what?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Lie—

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: With her?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: With her, on her; what you will.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Lie with her! lie on her! We say lie on her, when

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: they belie her. Lie with her! that’s fulsome.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: —Handkerchief—confessions—handkerchief!—To

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: confess, and be hanged for his labour;—first, to be

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: hanged, and then to confess.—I tremble at it.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Nature would not invest herself in such shadowing

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: passion without some instruction. It is not words

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: that shake me thus. Pish! Noses, ears, and lips.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: —Is’t possible?—Confess—handkerchief!—O devil!—

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Work on,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: My medicine, work! Thus credulous fools are caught;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And many worthy and chaste dames even thus,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: All guiltless, meet reproach. What, ho! my lord!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: My lord, I say! Othello!

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: What’s the matter?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: My lord is fall’n into an epilepsy:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: This is his second fit; he had one yesterday.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Rub him about the temples.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: No, forbear;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: The lethargy must have his quiet course:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: If not, he foams at mouth and by and by

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Breaks out to savage madness. Look he stirs:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Do you withdraw yourself a little while,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: He will recover straight: when he is gone,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I would on great occasion speak with you.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: How is it, general? have you not hurt your head?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Dost thou mock me?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I mock you! no, by heaven.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Would you would bear your fortune like a man!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: A horned man’s a monster and a beast.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: There’s many a beast then in a populous city,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And many a civil monster.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Did he confess it?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Good sir, be a man;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Think every bearded fellow that’s but yoked

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: May draw with you: there’s millions now alive

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That nightly lie in those unproper beds

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Which they dare swear peculiar: your case is better.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: O, ’tis the spite of hell, the fiend’s arch-mock,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: To lip a wanton in a secure couch,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And to suppose her chaste! No, let me know;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And knowing what I am, I know what she shall be.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: O, thou art wise; ’tis certain.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Stand you awhile apart;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Confine yourself but in a patient list.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Whilst you were here o’erwhelmed with your grief—

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: A passion most unsuiting such a man—

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Cassio came hither: I shifted him away,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And laid good ’scuse upon your ecstasy,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Bade him anon return and here speak with me;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: The which he promised. Do but encave yourself,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And mark the fleers, the gibes, and notable scorns,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That dwell in every region of his face;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: For I will make him tell the tale anew,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Where, how, how oft, how long ago, and when

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: He hath, and is again to cope your wife:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I say, but mark his gesture. Marry, patience;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Or I shall say you are all in all in spleen,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And nothing of a man.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Dost thou hear, Iago?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I will be found most cunning in my patience;

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: But—dost thou hear?—most bloody.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That’s not amiss;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: But yet keep time in all. Will you withdraw? Othello retires.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Now will I question Cassio of Bianca,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: A housewife that by selling her desires

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Buys herself bread and clothes: it is a creature

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That dotes on Cassio; as ’tis the strumpet’s plague

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: To beguile many and be beguiled by one:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: He, when he hears of her, cannot refrain

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: From the excess of laughter. Here he comes:

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: The worser that you give me the addition

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Whose want even kills me.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Ply Desdemona well, and you are sure on’t.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Speaking lower. Now, if this suit lay in Bianca’s power,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: How quickly should you speed!

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Alas, poor caitiff!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Look, how he laughs already!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I never knew woman love man so.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Alas, poor rogue! I think, i’ faith, she loves me.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Now he denies it faintly, and laughs it out.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Do you hear, Cassio?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Now he importunes him

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: To tell it o’er: go to; well said, well said.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: She gives it out that you shall marry hey:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Do you intend it?

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Ha, ha, ha!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Do you triumph, Roman? do you triumph?

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: I marry her! what? a customer! Prithee, bear some charity to my

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: wit: do not think it so unwholesome. Ha, ha, ha!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: So, so, so, so: they laugh that win.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: ’Faith, the cry goes that you shall marry her.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Prithee, say true.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I am a very villain else.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Have you scored me? Well.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: This is the monkey’s own giving out: she is persuaded I will

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: marry her, out of her own love and flattery, not out of

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: my promise.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Iago beckons me; now he begins the story.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: She was here even now; she haunts me in every place. I

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: was the other day talking on the sea-bank with certain Venetians; and

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: thither comes the bauble, and, by this hand, she falls me thus

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: about my neck—

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Crying “O dear Cassio!” as it were: his gesture imports it.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: So hangs, and lolls, and weeps upon me; so hales, and pulls

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: me: ha, ha, ha!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Now he tells how she plucked him to my chamber. O, I

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: see that nose of yours, but not that dog I shall throw

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: it to.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Well, I must leave her company.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Before me! look, where she comes.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: ’Tis such another fitchew! marry a perfumed one.

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: Let the devil and his dam haunt you! What did you mean

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: by that same handkerchief you gave me even now? I was a

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: fine fool to take it. I must take out the work?—A likely

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: piece of work, that you should find it in your chamber, and

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: not know who left it there! This is some minx’s token, and

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: I must take out the work? There; give it your hobby-horse: wheresoever

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: you had it, I’ll take out no work on’t.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: How now, my sweet Bianca! how now! how now!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: By heaven, that should be my handkerchief!

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: An you’ll come to supper to-night, you may; an you will not,

lines.speaker: BIANCA
lines.text: come when you are next prepared for.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: After her, after her.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: ’Faith, I must; she’ll rail in the street else.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Will you sup there?

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: ’Faith, I intend so.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Well, I may chance to see you; for I would very fain speak with you.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Prithee, come; will you?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Go to; say no more.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Advancing. How shall I murder him, Iago?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Did you perceive how he laughed at his vice?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: O Iago!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And did you see the handkerchief?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Was that mine?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Yours by this hand: and to see how he prizes the foolish

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: woman your wife! she gave it him, and he hath given it

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: his whore.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I would have him nine years a-killing. A fine woman! a fair

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: woman! a sweet woman!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Nay, you must forget that.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Ay, let her rot, and perish, and be damned to-night; for she

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: shall not live: no, my heart is turned to stone; I strike

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: it, and it hurts my hand. O, the world hath not a

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: sweeter creature: she might lie by an emperor’s side and command him

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: tasks.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Nay, that’s not your way.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Hang her! I do but say what she is: so delicate with

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: her needle: an admirable musician: O! she will sing the savageness out

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: of a bear: of so high and plenteous wit and invention:—

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: She’s the worse for all this.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: O, a thousand thousand times: and then, of so gentle a condition!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Ay, too gentle.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Nay, that’s certain: but yet the pity of it, Iago! O Iago,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: the pity of it, Iago!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: If you are so fond over her iniquity, give her patent to

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: offend; for, if it touch not you, it comes near nobody.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I will chop her into messes: cuckold me!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: O, ’tis foul in her.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: With mine officer!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That’s fouler.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Get me some poison, Iago; this night: I’ll not expostulate with her,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: lest her body and beauty unprovide my mind again: this night, Iago.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Do it not with poison, strangle her in her bed, even the

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: bed she hath contaminated.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Good, good: the justice of it pleases: very good.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And for Cassio, let me be his undertaker: you shall hear more by midnight.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Excellent good. A trumpet within. What trumpet is that same?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Something from Venice, sure. ’Tis Lodovico

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Come from the duke: and, see, your wife is with him.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Save you, worthy general!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: With all my heart, sir.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: The duke and senators of Venice greet you. Gives him a letter.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I kiss the instrument of their pleasures. Opens the letter, and reads.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: And what’s the news, good cousin Lodovico?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I am very glad to see you, signior;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Welcome to Cyprus.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: I thank you. How does Lieutenant Cassio?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Lives, sir.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Cousin, there’s fall’n between him and my lord

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: An unkind breach: but you shall make all well.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Are you sure of that?

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: My lord?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: “This fail you not to do, as you will—”

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: He did not call; he’s busy in the paper.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Is there division ’twixt my lord and Cassio?

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: A most unhappy one: I would do much

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: To atone them, for the love I bear to Cassio.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Fire and brimstone!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: My lord?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Are you wise?

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: What, is he angry?

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: May be the letter moved him;

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: For, as I think, they do command him home,

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Deputing Cassio in his government.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Trust me, I am glad on’t.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Indeed!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: My lord?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I am glad to see you mad.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Why, sweet Othello—

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Striking her. Devil!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: I have not deserved this.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: My lord, this would not be believed in Venice,

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Though I should swear I saw’t: ’tis very much:

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Make her amends; she weeps.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: O devil, devil!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: If that the earth could teem with woman’s tears,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Each drop she falls would prove a crocodile.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Out of my sight!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: I will not stay to offend you. Going.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Truly, an obedient lady:

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: I do beseech your lordship, call her back.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Mistress!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: My lord?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: What would you with her, sir?

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Who, I, my lord?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Ay; you did wish that I would make her turn:

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Sir, she can turn, and turn, and yet go on,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: And turn again; and she can weep, sir, weep;

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: And she’s obedient, as you say, obedient,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Very obedient. Proceed you in your tears.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Concerning this, sir—O well-painted passion!—

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I am commanded home. Get you away;

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I’ll send for you anon. Sir, I obey the mandate,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: And will return to Venice. Hence, avaunt!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Cassio shall have my place. And, sir, tonight,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I do entreat that we may sup together:

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: You are welcome, sir, to Cyprus.—Goats and monkeys!

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Is this the noble Moor whom our full senate

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Call all in all sufficient? Is this the nature

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Whom passion could not shake? whose solid virtue

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: The shot of accident, nor dart of chance,

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Could neither graze nor pierce?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: He is much changed.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Are his wits safe? is he not light of brain?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: He’s that he is: I may not breathe my censure

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: What he might be: if what he might he is not,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I would to heaven he were!

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: What, strike his wife!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: ’Faith, that was not so well; yet would I knew

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That stroke would prove the worst!

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Is it his use?

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Or did the letters work upon his blood,

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: And new-create this fault?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Alas, alas!

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: It is not honesty in me to speak

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: What I have seen and known. You shall observe him,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And his own courses will denote him so

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That I may save my speech: do but go after,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And mark how he continues.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: I am sorry that I am deceived in him.

stage_directions.text: As he shall smile, Othello shall go mad; And his unbookish jealousy must construe Poor Cassio’s smiles, gestures and light behavior, Quite in the wrong. How do you now, lieutenant?
What do you mean by this haunting of me?

glosses.definition: a kiss without license; an unsanctioned intimacy
hypocrisy that cheats the devil of his due — Iago's sly paradox
a quality invisible to the eye — honor
a raven croaks an omen over a plague-stricken house
truly, he did it — Iago's blunt lie
slander her
the handkerchief, now the fatal prop of the play — small linen cloth, heavy with meaning
gross; disgusting
Nature would not clothe herself in such a darkening passion without cause
seizure — thought in Shakespeare's time to be a form of the falling sickness
make a laughing stock of me

glosses.phrase: A horned man’s a monster and a beast
glosses.definition: a cuckold is both an unnatural creature and a brute animal
not exclusively their own
their own; private
within the limits of patience
a trance; a fit of madness

Iago's lethal counterfeit; the phrase drips with irony
Othello's cry at striking Desdemona in public — the most shocking stage moment in the play

habit; custom

annotations.title: Othello's trance as dramatic emblem
annotations.body:
Othello's physical collapse — the fit, the falling sickness (epilepsy as the Elizabethans understood it) — is Shakespeare's visible image for what the temptation scene has been doing invisibly. The general of Venice lies insensible on stage while his ensign watches. This is the first moment at which body gives ground to mind; up to now Othello's disintegration has been verbal. The stage picture is a diagnosis.

Shakespeare had used fits before — Caesar's in *Julius Caesar*, Macbeth's hallucinations — but neither was placed at the exact dramatic joint this is. The fall inverts Iago's humiliation of the general; the ensign stands, the commander is helpless. After this scene Othello will not recover strategic dignity; his authority becomes a public performance pitted against the private ruin.
sources: Robert B. Heilman, *Magic in the Web* (1956)
Margaret Healy, *Shakespeare, Alchemy and the Creative Imagination* (2011)

annotations.title: The eavesdropping and the audience's complicity
annotations.body:
In this scene Shakespeare gives the audience what it has not had before: Othello positioned to overhear Iago speak to Cassio, misreading every laugh and gesture. The dramatic irony tightens. The audience knows Cassio thinks he's being asked about Bianca; Othello thinks the subject is Desdemona. We see a double exposure: the real conversation and Othello's hallucination of it, simultaneously.

The device is brutally effective because it makes the audience complicit. We cannot both know what Othello sees and keep the peace with what Iago is doing; we are implicated in his method by the simple act of watching. *Othello* is Shakespeare's most sustained use of dramatic irony — not as a source of pleasure but as a source of moral weight on the spectator.

annotations.title: The pity of it, Iago
annotations.body:
'But yet the pity of it, Iago — O, Iago, the pity of it, Iago.' The line is a dramatic aberration. Surrounded by the hardening of Othello's murder plan, it registers the cost of what he is about to do. The repetition — 'pity', 'Iago', three times — reads as a man hearing himself from outside, one foot still in the country he is leaving.

Shakespeare refuses to resolve the moment into either penitence or resolve. Othello will proceed to plan the killing in the same scene. But the line is not mere residue. It is the play's proof that Othello is not numb to what he is about to do — which makes the tragedy worse, not milder.

annotations.title: Striking Desdemona in public
annotations.body:
The blow is the most transgressive stage moment in Shakespearean tragedy. The Venetian ambassador Lodovico is present; he is a state witness; the violence is public and unprovoked and registered immediately as scandal. The line that follows — Lodovico's astonished 'My lord, this would not be believed in Venice, / Though I should swear I saw't' — is Shakespeare's blunt acknowledgment of how the scene works. It lives on the far side of plausibility.

What makes it work dramatically is the presence of witnesses. Othello does not strike Desdemona in private rage; he does it in full senatorial view. The collapse of his public self is now complete. What Iago has done to Othello's private mind is now visible to the state. The rest of the play is the working out, in public, of a ruin that happened in private.

trials.prompt: Why does Iago arrange for Othello to overhear his conversation with Cassio?
options: To clear Cassio of any wrongdoing through direct confrontation
To let Othello misinterpret laughter about Bianca as admissions about Desdemona — using Othello's distance to weaponize ambiguity
To resolve a financial dispute between Cassio and Bianca
To gather evidence that will clear Desdemona's name

trials.prompt: What does the audience know during the eavesdropping scene that Othello does not?
options: That Cassio is secretly in love with Desdemona
That Cassio's laughter and boasting concern Bianca, not Desdemona
That the handkerchief has been destroyed
That Iago is Cassio's secret ally

trials.prompt: When Othello repeats 'But yet the pity of it, Iago — O, Iago, the pity of it, Iago', the tone is:
options: Triumphant — he has achieved the certainty he sought
Anguished — he hears himself from outside, one foot still in the country he is leaving
Sarcastic — he is mocking Iago's earlier expressions of pity
Detached — he has become indifferent to Desdemona

trials.prompt: Othello strikes Desdemona in front of the Venetian ambassador Lodovico. Why is this the play's most transgressive stage moment?
options: Because striking a wife was illegal in Venice but common in Cyprus
Because the private ruin of Othello's mind is now witnessed officially and the public self collapses in full senatorial view
Because Desdemona's relatives in Venice will now seek revenge
Because it violates an unwritten military code

trials.prompt: What message does Lodovico bring from Venice?
options: Orders to execute Iago for treason
A letter recalling Othello to Venice and appointing Cassio as governor of Cyprus
News that Desdemona's father Brabantio has died
A warning that the Turkish fleet has regrouped
